page.title: Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella 2026: Superyachts & Crewed Charters

data.hero_alt: Luxury superyacht moored at Puerto Banús with the harbour buildings behind
data.meta_description: Luxury yacht charter Marbella from €749 on our 12.5 m Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 motor yachts — licensed skipper, drinks and VAT included. Crewed charters from Puerto Banús.
data.summary: Luxury and superyacht charter spoke: vessel sizes, crew configuration, day vs weekly rates, what crewed actually means, deposit and process.
key_facts: Luxury yacht charter Marbella from €2,400 for a full day on an 18-20 m crewed yacht
Superyachts 24 m+ run €4,500-€18,000 per day depending on length and crew size
Weekly charters on 25 m+ yachts start at €25,000 plus expenses (APA model)
Standard crew: captain + stewardess on 18 m; captain + mate + chef + stewardess on 24 m+
Puerto Banús is the only Marbella marina that routinely berths superyachts above 24 m

faq.q: What counts as a luxury yacht in Marbella?
faq.a: Generally 18 metres and above, with a dedicated crew of at least captain plus stewardess, and an interior fit-out aimed at hospitality rather than transport. Below 18 m it's still a private yacht but the experience is more 'private boat' than 'luxury'. Above 24 m you're in proper superyacht territory with a chef and multiple staterooms.

faq.q: How much is a luxury yacht charter per day?
faq.a: Day charters: €2,400 for an 18 m crewed yacht in low season, €3,400 in August. A 22-24 m yacht runs €4,000-€6,500 a day. Superyachts 28-30 m sit at €8,000-€18,000. Weekly: starts around €25,000 for a 24 m yacht and climbs steeply. Fuel beyond the standard coastal cruise, marina fees in foreign ports and provisioning are extras (APA: Advance Provisioning Allowance, typically 25-30% on top).

Captain handles navigation, anchoring and decision-making. Stewardess handles guest service: drinks, food plating, cabin turn-down on overnight charters. On 24 m+ yachts a chef cooks every meal from a customised menu, and a deckhand or mate keeps the boat immaculate. Tipping the crew is customary — 10-15% of charter price, split among the team, paid in cash at the end.

Within reason. Standard day charters cruise the Costa del Sol from Estepona to Cabopino. Longer day trips can reach Gibraltar (90-100 NM round trip, see our Gibraltar day trip guide), or push east to Málaga. Overnight charters open up the Strait of Gibraltar, Tarifa, and even short Morocco crossings. Routes outside Spanish waters require additional paperwork — captain handles it.

faq.q: How far ahead do I need to book?
faq.a: Day charters: 2-4 weeks for July-August Saturdays on the most popular yachts; 5-7 days for shoulder season weekdays. Weekly charters on specific yachts in August: 3-6 months in advance is normal. The market for 30 m+ yachts is small (maybe 8-10 vessels regularly in Marbella) so peak weeks sell out early.

data.body_html:
Our fleet right now: two 12.5 m flybridge motor yachts — the Astondoa 40 (9 guests) and Azimut 39 (11 guests). From €749 for 2h, up to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day. Skipper, fuel, drinks, insurance and VAT included.

Luxury yacht rental in Marbella means stepping onto an 18-to-30-metre crewed yacht at Puerto Banús, being greeted by a stewardess with a chilled glass, and handing the day over to a professional crew while you and your guests do nothing but enjoy it. Day charters start at €2,400 and scale up to €18,000 for the largest superyachts. Weekly charters begin around €25,000 plus expenses.

Where the line is

Yacht charter in Marbella exists on a continuum, and "luxury" is the top end of it. Practical breakpoints:

  • 10-15 m motor yacht: private day boat. Captain only. Comfortable but not luxury. Covered on the yacht charter Marbella page.
  • 18-22 m crewed yacht: entry-level luxury. Captain + stewardess. Hospitality-fitted interior, real galley, fold-out swim platform, water toys included. This is where most luxury bookings sit.
  • 24-30 m yacht: superyacht. Full crew (captain, mate, chef, stewardess). Multiple staterooms, chef-prepared meals, on-board tender. Day charter possible but most owners prefer multi-day.
  • 30 m+ megayacht: charter market thins out fast. Usually weekly or longer, crew of 5-10, full-board.

Our fleet — luxury-tier amenities at the 12.5 m size class

We currently run two crewed 12.5 m flybridge motor yachts at €749 → €2,299 per charter — both with cream-leather interiors, AC saloon, two cabins, and the same skipper-fuel-drinks-insurance-VAT inclusion as larger luxury yachts. See Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 for full specs. For 18 m+ proper superyachts (weekly charters, APA model, full crew with chef) we can source through partners — message us with dates and group size.

Weekly charter and the APA model

Multi-day and weekly charters on 24 m+ yachts use the global APA system. The base charter fee covers the yacht, captain and crew salaries. On top of that you pre-pay an Advance Provisioning Allowance (APA) of 25-30% which funds fuel, marina fees, food, drink and any extra crew expenses during the trip. Captain accounts for every euro and refunds the unused portion at the end. A 24 m yacht for a week in low season: ~€25,000 + ~€7,000 APA. Same yacht in August: ~€40,000 + €10,000 APA.

The crew and what they actually do

Captain: commands the yacht — navigation, anchoring, all decisions affecting safety. Holds at least a 200 GT commercial licence. Your single point of contact for the trip.

Stewardess: guest service. Welcomes you on board, mixes drinks, plates the food, handles cabin turn-down on overnight charters, keeps the saloon and decks tidy. The stewardess role on a luxury yacht is the difference between "nice boat" and "luxury experience".

Chef: from 24 m up. You fill in a preference sheet before the charter — favourite cuisines, dietary requirements, allergies — and the chef builds a tailored menu for the trip. Provisioning is done from local markets (Mercado de Puerto Banús, La Cañada).

Deckhand / mate: handles lines, fenders, tender operation, water-toys deployment, and keeps the exterior immaculate. Often a deck cadet building sea time toward their captain qualification.

Where you can go

Day charters cover the Costa del Sol — Sotogrande in the west, Cabopino in the east, with the Golden Mile coast in between. A long day with an early start reaches Gibraltar by boat from Marbella (100 NM round trip, only viable on flat-sea days). Overnight luxury charters extend the cruising window along the Marbella coast and the Strait. Weekly bookings out of Marbella typically anchor the itinerary around Puerto Banús and the Golden Mile stops most guests want to see.

The booking process

  1. WhatsApp inquiry — dates, group size, length (day/weekend/week), special requirements.
  2. Shortlist — we send 2-4 specific yachts with photos, plans, crew bios, day rates and any APA estimates.
  3. Hold — preferred yacht is held for 48 hours while you decide.
  4. Contract — MYBA Worldwide standard charter agreement, 50% deposit at signing.
  5. Preference sheet — sent 2-3 weeks ahead, captures food/drink/itinerary preferences.
  6. Boarding — exact pier number sent 24 hours ahead, balance paid on or before the day.

For the full picture of what else is available across the fleet, see the boat rental Marbella hub.

Frequently asked questions

What counts as a luxury yacht in Marbella?

Generally 18 metres and above with captain plus stewardess minimum, hospitality-fitted interior, and water toys. Above 24 m you're in superyacht territory with chef and multiple staterooms.

How much is a luxury yacht charter per day?

€2,400 for an 18 m yacht in low season, up to €18,000 for a megayacht in August. Weekly starts ~€25,000 for a 24 m yacht plus APA expenses.

What does 'crewed charter' actually include?

Captain (navigation), stewardess (guest service), chef from 24 m up (custom menus), deckhand (lines, tender, water toys). Tipping 10-15% of charter price at the end is customary.

Can we go anywhere?

Day charters cover Estepona to Cabopino along the Marbella coast. Long days reach Gibraltar (see our dedicated guide). Overnight and weekly charters extend the cruising window along the Costa del Sol.

How far ahead do I need to book?

Day charters: 2-4 weeks for peak Saturdays. Weekly charters on specific yachts in August: 3-6 months. The 30 m+ market is small (8-10 yachts) and sells out early.
